$comment: Reusable secret-isolation fixture (KRT-BD003). Stages representative, non-real provider and MCP credentials confined to the integration edge. The KRT-BD004 secret-isolation check set configures these on a turn and the shared runner-owned secret-absence helper asserts none of `secretValues` (or their common encoded variants) appear in persisted kernel records, captured canonical stream events, captured telemetry attributes or error summaries, or the recorded transcript. `bridgeRequestToken` (KRT-BK004) is a JWT-shaped, pattern-detectable secret distinct from the four value-equality secrets above: it is used by the provider-bridge secret-isolation check set to prove the new `secretPatternAbsence` structural assertion — which recognizes secret-*shaped* values by pattern, not just exact configured strings — actually detects a credential-shaped leak class.
